text: The surgeon robotically mobilizes the small bowel out of the pelvis and prepares the rectum. The efferent limb of the descending colon is dissected and freed from the abdominal wall in a morbidly obese patient undergoing colostomy takedown after perforated diverticulitis.
The surgeon uses robotic scissors to sharply amputate a section of tissue.

The surgeon uses cautery with a cut setting to perform the procedure, while employing Firefly technology to assess tissue perfusion. The bedside assistant applies pressure to the abdominal wall, although this action is not visible on screen.

The surgeon positions the VesselSealer Xtend at the inferior border below the skin within the peristomal hernia. The assistant has already placed the anvil through the colostomy and inserted a suture, allowing the surgeon to run a running purse-string suture using the instrument.
The surgeon uses a needle driver to perform a running purse-string suture, demonstrating improved control and precision compared to the VesselSealer.
The surgeon completes the running purse string suture around the anvil, which has been previously dropped into the abdomen, and then places the anvil intraluminally, cinching the suture tightly.
The surgeon has dissected and prepared the rectum distally, and is now using robotic scissors to sharply amputate the rectum.
The EEA stapler is positioned transanally, upstream from the proctotomy site, while the rectum has been sized and dilated.
The surgeon begins running a purse string suture using a V-lock suture, starting from the outer edge and moving inward, then reversing the direction from inward to outward.

The surgeon extrudes the anvil spike completely from the lumen of the rectum, utilizing a thirty-degree upward view on the X-eye to assist in completing the purse string suture on the posterior edge of the proctotomy.
The surgeon cinches down the purse string suture and cuts it, advancing the taper further. The anvil is snapped into place, resulting in the retrieval of two robust, healthy anastomotic doughnuts.
